english: removed, of diseases Lt., opp. to rgyas (rgyas) and bsags (bsags).
pf. p'yó (phyo), p'yós (phyos), imp. p'yo (phyo), byo (byo), byos (byos), to pour out, to pour into another vessel, to transfuse Lex. and Cs.
pf. byogs (byogs) to lick Lex. and Cs.

I. pf byáṅ-ba (byang ba) 1. to be cleansed, purified, v. byáṅ-ba (byang ba). — 2. to be skilled, well versed, rig-byéd-la (rig byed la) in the Vedas Tar. — II. pf _obyoṅs-pa ('byongs pa) to be finished, perfect, complete, frq. with snyiṅ-rje (snying rje) Mil and elsewh., to exercise full compassion(?) cf. sbyóṅ-ba (sbyong ba). (The above arrangement is nothing more than an attempt; in order to arrive at any certainty as to these roots, a far greater number of observations would be required.)

pf. and imp. byon (byon), resp. to go, proceed, travel, dé-nas byón-pa-na (de nas byon pa na) then in proceeding on the way Glr.; to arrive, appear, become visible; also for _obyúṅ-ba ('byung ba), e.g. raṅ-byón (rang byon); with root of the verb: p'úr-byon-pas (phur byon pas) preparing to fly Mil.; ma-byón-pa (ma byon pa) = ma-óṅs-pa (ma 'ongs pa) future (Buddhas) S.O.; to rise, to appear; with dat. inf. = júg-pa ('jug pa) to begin, to set about a certain work Tar. 125, 16.

wealth, riches, goods, treasures, _obyor-pa ('byor pa) zád-mi-śes-pa (zad mi shes pa) daṅ (dang) ldán-pa (ldan pa) one possessing inexhaustible wealth, bde-ba (bde ba) daṅ (dang) _obyór-pa ('byor pa) joy and treasures S.O.; _obyór-pa ('byor pa) drug (drug) Pth., prob. six kinds of temporal goods; raṅ-gi (rang gi) _obyór-pa ('byor pa) lṅa (lnga) and gżan-gyi (gzhan gyi) _obyór-pa ('byor pa) lṅa (lnga) five subjective and five objective goods, of a similar nature as those mentioned sub dal-_obyor (dal 'byor), yet without any evident reason for being thus divided Thgy.; _obyor-ldán ('byor ldan) rich, mostly used as a noun personal.

I. intrs. of sbyór-ba (sbyor ba) 1. to stick to, adhere to Med.; *k'yág-la (khyag la) jar (jar) soṅ* (song), it is frozen fast W.; _obyár-byed ('byar byed) spyin (spyin) glue Lex.; _obyor-sman ('byor sman) sticking-plaster W; to infect, of diseases, _obyor-nad ('byor nad) an infectious disease Cs. also mentally: *lo (lo) or sém-la (sems la) jar* (jar) it sticks fast, is remembered, borne in mind. 2. to be prepared, ready, at hand, extant, śa (sha) ma (ma) byór-nas (byor nas) there being no meat prepared Dzl.; p'rál-du (phral du) _obyór-ba ('byor ba) ma (ma) yin (yin) that is not at once in readiness Dzl.; ći (ci) _obyór-ba ('byor ba) des (des) mčód-pa (mchod pa) byéd-pa (byed pa) to offer sacrifice of such things as are at hand Dzl.; ći-ste (ci ste) _obyór-bar ('byor bar) mi (mi) _ogyúr-na ('gyur na) but if he has not such a thing at his disposal Sambh. — 3. to agree, mi-_obyór-ba (mi 'byor ba) k'á-čig (kha cig) some disagreements, contradictions Tar. -- II. resp. to come, arrive, W., C.; *k'yé?-kyi (khyed kyi) ku (sku) dún-du (mdun du) jàr-gyu ('byor rgyu) yin* (yin) I shall appear before your Honour C.; *nyúr-du (myur du) jar ('byor) yoṅ* (yong) I shall immediately attend C.

pf. and imp. byol (byol), fut. (and pres. in C.) dbyol (dbyol) to give or make way, to turn out of the way, to step aside, gćig-gis (gcig gis) gćig-la (gcig la) Dzl.; _obyól-te ('byol te) gro ('gro) in walking I make way (to people) Dzl.; W. with accus.: *rul (sbrul), las (las), díg-pa (sdig pa) jól-će* ('byol ce) to step out of the way of, to shun, a serpent, toil, sin. Sometimes jól-ba ('byol ba).

1. pf. _obraṅs ('brangs), imp. _obroṅ ('brong), to bear, bring forth, give birth; to litter, bráṅ-mo (brang mo) an animal going with young, bearing Cs. — 2. also _obréṅ-ba ('breng ba), pf. _obraṅs ('brangs), imp. _obreṅs ('brengs) Mil. (_obroṅ ('brong) Sch?) to follow, to walk at another's heels, with pyir (phyir), pyí-bżin (phyi bzhin) (-du (-du)), rjés-su (rjes su), W. *tiṅ-la* (rting la) with genit., to follow, pursue, hunt after, dbyúg-pas (dbyug pas) with a stick Pth.; to pursue, in one's thoughts.
pf. brad (brad), imp. brod (brod) to scratch, to scrape, with the nails, claws etc.; to lacerate by scratching, gdoṅ (gdong) Dzl.; also to gnaw, nibble at.

pf. brab (brab), imp. brob (brob) 1. to catch suddenly, to snap away, snatch away, a fly with one's hand, the prey with a bound. — 2. to beat, to scourge, ts'er-mág-gis (tsher mag gis) with thorns Thgy. — 3. to throw out, to scatter, magical objects, such as grains of barley etc.
